In the actual working of the directions, the entire empha
sis shifts to the aspects formed between the radical and pro
gressed positions. No aspect is good or bld in itself. Its precise
nature depends on the nature of the planets, on their relation
to the ascendant, and on their location. Next we have to
consider the observation of Robert Yaget that the actual event
may not take place exactly at the time when an aspect is
formed. This he designates the time-lag. ln other words every
aspect has a certain orb. It extends. If it extends in one
direction, it can as well extend backwards. Then the period
covered by an orb will be something like a vidasa or a minor
period within a sub-period. Can we overcome the conception of
a time-lag?
Whatever system of directions we like to perfect, we have
to keep in mind two factors of paramount importance in any
prognostication. The first is the dasa system of 120 years
which in itself is a system of directions. This is of great help in
